data.title: Randomized Controlled Study on the Evaluation of the T-PEP Method Based on Positive Expiratory Pressure Versus Intrapulmonary Percussive Ventilation for Lower Respiratory Airways Clearance in Sub-acute, Tetraplegic, Tracheotomized Spinal Cord Injured Patients
data.brief_summary: In tetraplegic patients with complete cervical spinal cord injury, respiratory complications are very frequent, especially in the sub-acute phase: the lungs often become obstructed due to the accumulation of secretions and the contemporary inefficiency of the cough mechanism. The present pilot study aims, in the context of a rehabilitative Critical Care Unit, at evaluating a not yet published method, called "T-PEP" and based on the principle of Positive Expiratory Pressure, applicable to tracheo
